gdp.notes: Consumer spending remains the primary driver, with services outpacing goods. Business investment in AI infrastructure is an emerging tailwind.

inflation.notes: Inflation continues to trend downward from 2022 peaks but remains above the Fed's 2% target. Shelter costs and services inflation are sticky.

interest_rates.notes: Yield curve remains inverted since mid-2022. Market pricing suggests first rate cut in June 2025. Fed maintaining data-dependent stance.

employment.notes: Labor market remains resilient with strong job creation. Wage growth is gradually decelerating. Tech sector layoffs contrast with broad market strength.

housing.notes: Elevated mortgage rates continue to suppress housing affordability. Inventory remains low, keeping prices sticky despite low transaction volumes.

consumer.notes: Consumer sentiment is improving but remains below pre-pandemic levels. Spending is rotating from goods to services. Credit card delinquencies are ticking up.
